 correct Hess cycle with arrow on the RHS going down and correct balanced products in the box.  calculation of left-hand side  Correct and sign (1) (1) (1) Example of calculation 2C (s,graphite) + 2H (g) + ½ O (g) → CH CHO(g) (+2 ½ O ) (+2 ½ O ) Ignore state symbols even if incorrect No TE on incorrect cycle (2 x − 286) + (2 x − 394) = − 1360 (kJ mol − ) An expression giving − 1360 (kJ mol − ) is sufficient ( − 1360 (kJ mol − ) + 1167 (kJ mol − )) = − 193 (kJ mol − ) If units are given they must be correct Allow kJ mol − for kJ mol − Correct answer with or without working scores M2 and M3 +193 (kJ mol − ) scores M2 TE on LHS (3) (Total for Question 19 = 16 Marks) 2CO (g) + 2H O (l) Number Additional Guidance Mark  time taken for the (first appearance of the )precipitate (of silver halide) to form Allow ppt / ppte for precipitate / cloudy/ silver halide Allow how fast / how quickly / rate for time (1) Number Additional Guidance Mark An explanation that makes reference to the following points:  1-iodopropane  C − I bond weakest/lowest bond enthalpy (1) (1) Allow any correct formula Do not award for simply saying the iodine /1-iodopropane bond is weakest M2 depends on M1 Ignore reference to bond length Ignore any references to reactivity, electronegativity/size (2) Number Additional Mark 20 (b) A mechanism that shows:  dipole on C-Cl bond and curly arrow from bond to Cl or just beyond  curly arrow from lone pair on OH – ion to δ + carbon (1) (1) Ignore S N 2 transition state Do not award curly arrow from negative charge on OH Do not award M2 if a metal hydroxide e.g. KOH has a covalent bond Ignore products Penalise use of half arrows once only in M1 and M2 If S N mechanism M1 as above and then M2 awarded for curly arrow from lone pair on OH – ion to C+ of carbocation (2) Number Additional Guidance Mark

An answer that makes refere nce to the following points:  London forces (and (permanent) dipole-(permanent) dipole forces)  iodine atoms are more polarisable (than chlorine or bromine)/ 1-iodopropane has more electrons (than 1- chloropropane and 1-bromopropane)  (resulting in) strong er / more London Forces (so more energy required to overcome these forces)  (despite) 1-iodopropane having the weakest (permanent) dipole / (permanent) dipole forces (1) (1) (1) (1) Accept dispersion forces / instantaneous dipole- (induced) dipole Allow van der Waals’ Allow iodine has more electrons than chlorine/ bromine Ignore it is a larger molecu le / larger molar mass Do not award iodine has stronger/more London forces. Allow van der Waals / dispersion forces Ignore just stronger in termolecular forces Do not award M3 if any indication that covalent bonds are being broken. Allow 1-chloropropane has the strongest (permanent) dipole / (per manent) dipole forces Ignore abbreviations such as id-id and pd-pd (4) Number Additional Guidance Mark 20 (d) Skeletal formul a Name Z- but-2-ene E -but-2-ene but-1-ene Allow any order Allow cis Allow trans If only one but-2-ene isomer is given the E/Z does not have to be identified Penalise displayed formulae only once All 6 correct scores (3) 4-5 correct scores (2) 2-3 correct scores (1) No TE of names of incorrect formulae (3) ( Total for Question 20 = 12 Marks) Number Additional Guidance Mark  correct equation  correct state symbols (1) (1) Examples of equations 2K(s) + 2H O(l) 2KOH(aq) + H (g) OR K(s) + H O(l) KOH(aq) +½H (g) Allow multiples M2 dependent on M1 or correct species and an unbalanced equatio n (2) Number Additional Guidance Mark 21 (a)(ii) An answer that makes reference to the following points:  K / potassium from 0 to +1 and oxidation  H / hydrogen from +1 to 0 and reduction (1) (1) If oxidation numbers are wrong or omitted one mark is scored for K oxidised and H reduced. or one mark is scored for K changes from 0 to +1 a

A description that makes reference to the following points:  (some) oil weighed with M/ the mass of M is lower than the mass of material weighed/  relative atomic mass of M will be greater (1) (1) Ignore mass increased/decr eased/mass different Allow fewer moles of HCl (required in the titration)/lower titration value (2) (Total for Question 21 = 13 Marks) (Total for Section B = 41 Marks) Section C Number Additional Guidance Mark

A description that makes refere nce to the following points:  advantage: (it is produced from) renewable / sustainable (resources) or it produces fewer emissions (of CO / greenhouse gases)  disadvantage: it uses up la nd (that could be used for food production)/uses a f ood crop that could be eaten or it produces less energy (per mole / g) (1) (1) Do not award no greenhouse gases Ignore cleaner fuel/ more efficient combustion Ignore reference to engine modifications/carbon neutrality/using less oil/ good for the environment Ignore just ‘ it uses lots of crops’ Ignore references to time to grow crops/time to carry out fermentation/cost Ignore produces CO Do not award any referen ces to ozone depletion (2) Number Additional Guidance Mark

 ethanol can be oxidised Allow ethanol can react with oxygen Allow possible (organic) products of oxidation e.g. carboxylic acid / ethanoic ac id / aldehyde / ethanal Ignore just ‘oxidation take s place’ or ‘it can be oxidised’ Allow to ensure only anae robic respiration takes place. Allow aerobic respiration would take place (in the air) (producing water an d carbon dioxide) Do not award yeast reacts with oxygen Do not award to prevent combustion/ethanol would b urn (1) Number Additional Guidance Mark

 (fractional) distillation Allow distil the mixture (1) Number Additional Guidance Mark  ethanol forms hydrogen bonds (with water) Ignore both are polar/ they are miscible Ignore comments about so lubility/lik e dissolves like (1) Number Additional Guidance Mark An answer that makes reference to one of the following points:  (water can cause) corrosion / rusting  reduce the energy efficiency of the engine/fuel/ethanol  causes phase separation of the fuel Ignore damages the engine Allow water does not burn/ fewer km per l / miles per gallon Allow just ‘reduced efficiency’ Ignore references to et hanol being diluted (1) Numbe r Additional Guidance Mark *22(e)(i) This question assesses the student’s ability to show a coherent and logically structured Answ er with linkages and fully sustained reasoning. Marks are awarded for indicat ive content and for how the Answer is structured and shows lines of reasoning. The following table shows how th e Marks should be awarded for indicative content. Number of indicative Marking points seen in Answer Number of Marks awarded for indicative Marking points 5-4 3-2 1 1 0 0 The following table shows how th e Marks should be awarded for structure and lines of reasoning. Number of Marks awarded for structure of Answer and sustained lines of reasonin g Answer shows a coherent logical structure with linkages and fully sustained lines of reasoning demonstrated throu g hout Guidance on how the Mark scheme should be applied. The Mark for indicati ve content should be added to the Mark for lines of reasoning. For example, a response with five indicative Marking points that is partially structured with some linkages and lines of reasoning scores 4 Marks (3 Marks for indicative content and 1 Mark for partial structure and some linkages and lines of reasoning).
